channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania launches initiative to train more AI and Data Science professionals
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has unveiled a national drive to expand the number of professionals in Information and Communication Technology (ICT), Artificial Intelligence (AI), and Data Science. The in...

item.title: KNCHR Clarifies Objectives of Reparations for Protest Victims in Kenya
item.description: The Kenya National Commission on Human Rights (KNCHR) has clarified that the upcoming reparations for victims of protest-related violence are aimed at achieving justice rather than just financial payo...

item.title: Federal Constabulary to be deployed in Balochistan to strengthen security and law enforcement
item.description: The federal and provincial governments of Pakistan have agreed on a phased deployment of the Federal Constabulary in Balochistan to reinforce law and order in the province. The decision was made in a ...

item.title: Around 200 U.S. Troops Injured as Iran Conflict Enters Third Week
item.description: The number of United States military personnel wounded in the ongoing conflict with Iran has reached approximately 200, according to the U.S. Central Command. Most of the injured troops sustained mino...

item.title: Philippines Reaffirms Sovereignty Over Scarborough Shoal Amid China Dispute
item.description: The Philippines has strongly rejected claims by China over Bajo de Masinloc, also known as Scarborough Shoal, in the South China Sea. The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) emphasized that Philippine...

item.title: Calgary, Edmonton, and Prague Selected as Hosts for 2028 World Cup of Hockey
item.description: The 2028 World Cup of Hockey will be jointly hosted by Calgary, Edmonton, and Prague, marking a significant international collaboration in the world of ice hockey. This announcement highlights Canada’...

item.title: How Formula 1’s 2026 Sustainability Rules Are Shaping Race Strategy
item.description: The 2026 Formula 1 season has introduced substantial rule changes aimed at enhancing sustainability and overtaking. At the Australian Grand Prix, the number of on-track overtakes nearly tripled from t...

item.title: Supriya Sule confirms NCP (SP) will back Sunetra Pawar in Baramati bypoll
item.description: Nationalist Congress Party (Sharad Pawar faction) working president Supriya Sule announced on Monday that her party would not field a candidate against Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Sunetra Pawar ...

item.title: Divergence Between Paper and Physical Oil Prices Amid Middle East Conflict Expectations
item.description: The article examines the current state of the global oil market, highlighting a significant divergence between futures prices traded on exchanges and actual physical cargo prices. Oil, like stocks, is...

item.title: Dar es Salaam Maritime Institute to move to Kimbiji to allow new port berth construction
item.description: Tanzania government don announce plans to relocate Dar es Salaam Maritime Institute (DMI) from its current site to a bigger 13-acre place in Kimbiji, Kigamboni District. This move go make way for buil...

item.title: County Grammar School Old Boys Elect New National Executive to Lead for Three Years
item.description: The Old Boys Association of County Grammar School, Ikwerre/Etche, Rivers State, don elect new national executive committee wey go manage the association for the next three years. This one happen for t...

item.title: UK Government Offers £3,000 Incentive to Employers Hiring Young Jobseekers
item.description: The UK government has announced a new initiative granting a £3,000 bonus to businesses that hire young people who have been unemployed for at least six months. Work and Pensions Secretary Pat McFadden...

item.title: India Responds to USCIRF Report, Rejects Allegations of Religious Freedom Violations
item.description: India has officially rejected the recent report released by the US Commission on International Religious Freedom (USCIRF), which criticised the country for alleged violations of religious freedom and ...

item.title: Megalotto Jackpot of P20.8 Million Claimed by Single Winner
item.description: A lucky bettor successfully won the Megalotto 6/45 jackpot prize of P20,803,484.37 in the draw held on Monday night, March 16, 2026, according to the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO). The ...

item.title: Kent Meningitis Outbreak Highlights Risks for Young People
item.description: A meningitis outbreak in Kent has resulted in the deaths of two students—a sixth-form pupil in Canterbury and a university student at the University of Kent—and left 11 others seriously ill. Health au...

item.title: Analysis of the Upcoming Five State Assembly Elections in April 2026
item.description: The Election Commission of India (ECI) has announced the schedule for five Assembly elections to be held in April 2026. West Bengal will have a two-phase election, a significant reduction from eight p...

item.title: Trump Faces Challenges in Gaining International Support Amid Iran Crisis
item.description: The article analyses the unfolding conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran, emphasising the unexpected difficulties facing President Trump. Initially, Trump appeared confident that swift ...

item.title: Canada Holds Deportation Hearing for Former Senior Iranian Official
item.description: A deportation hearing has started in Canada for Abbas Omidi, a 55-year-old man alleged to have been a senior official in Iran’s Ministry of Industry, Mine and Trade. According to the Canada Border Ser...

item.title: US Stocks Rise on AI Sector Gains and Tech Announcements
item.description: US equities ended the session higher, driven by a resurgence in technology and AI-related shares. Nvidia saw significant gains after CEO Jensen Huang unveiled new components at the company’s annual de...

item.title: Nairobi Government Launches 48-Hour Plan to Address Flood Damage
item.description: Nairobi city government, in partnership with the national government, has launched an urgent 48-hour action plan to tackle the ongoing flooding affecting various parts of the capital. Governor Johnson...
